title: Elevate Easter treats with Rooibos
description: LIFESTYLE NEWS - As Easter approaches, local...
bodyText:
LIFESTYLE NEWS - As Easter approaches, local chefs and food artisans are embracing the versatility of Rooibos, infusing this indigenous tisane into an array of innovative culinary creations.

From traditional desserts to avant-garde confections, Rooibos is making a flavourful impact on the gastronomic scene.

Adele du Toit, spokesperson for the South African Rooibos Council, highlights the growing trend. “Rooibos has transcended its role as a mere beverage. Its unique flavour profile and health benefits have inspired chefs to experiment, resulting in dishes that are both delicious and reflective of our rich heritage.”


> Rooibos-infused Easter delights


Easter celebrations are synonymous with indulgent treats.

This year, Rooibos is taking centre stage in festive recipes:

Rooibos, Honey and Vanilla Easter Cake:This moist cake combines the earthy notes of Rooibos with the sweetness of honey and vanilla, topped with a luscious cream cheese icing. It's adorned with speckled eggs, making it a visual and gastronomic delight for Easter gatherings. View the recipe here.

Photo: Food & Home

Rooibos Chai & Honey Hot Cross Buns:A twist on the classic, these buns are infused with Rooibos chai, offering a spiced, aromatic flavour that complements the traditional Easter treat. Give the recipe a try.

Photo: Side Chef


> Unconventional Rooibos creations


Chefs are pushing the boundaries by incorporating Rooibos into inventive culinary domains:

Rooibos Crème Brûlée:A South African spin on the French classic, this dessert features a silky custard infused with Rooibos, topped with a caramelised sugar crust.

Rooibos “Tea”-ramisu:Reimagining the Italian favourite, this dessert layers biscuits dipped in Rooibos with a Rooibos-infused mascarpone mixture, finished with chocolate shavings.

Photo: Unilever


> The art of pairing Rooibos and chocolate


The harmonious blend of Rooibos and chocolate is also gaining in popularity.

“Both Rooibos and chocolate are associated with comfort and indulgence, aligning perfectly with the spirit of Easter. Enjoying them together enhances these sentiments,” says du Toit.

For more on how to pair Rooibos and chocolate, click here.

Chocolatiers are crafting Rooibos-infused truffles and hot beverages, offering consumers a novel tasting experience that marries the tisane’s subtle notes with rich cocoa flavours. Try this delicious Rooibos Chocolate Latte.

“The movement to incorporate indigenous ingredients like Rooibos reflects a broader trend in South African cuisine, where chefs are championing local flavours that resonate with cultural significance and contemporary tastes – a trend richly illustrated in the Edible Fynbos website's Guide to Using Edible Fynbos, which showcases a vibrant flavour wheel and practical pairing ideas for integrating native botanicals into modern cooking.”

As Rooibos continues to inspire culinary innovation, it not only enriches South African gastronomy, but also invites food enthusiasts to explore and celebrate the nation's rich culinary heritage.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
